categories.name: Machine Learning
categories.description: Classical ML algorithms applied to real business problems.

categories.name: Deep Learning
categories.description: Neural network architectures for vision, language, and sequence data.

categories.name: Automation
categories.description: Industry automation that removes manual, repetitive work.

projects.title: House Price Prediction
projects.short_description: Predicts house sale price from property features.
projects.problem_statement: Estimate fair market value of a house before listing.
projects.dataset_description: Ames/Boston-style housing dataset with size, location, and condition features.
projects.business_use_case: Helps agents and sellers price listings competitively instead of guessing.
projects.workflow_steps: Collect data -> Clean & impute -> EDA on price drivers -> Feature engineering (age, price/sqft) -> Train Linear Regression baseline -> Regularize with Ridge/Lasso -> Evaluate -> Deploy
projects.results: R2 = 0.87, RMSE reduced 18% vs. mean-price baseline.
projects.key_learning: Regularization mattered more than adding features once multicollinearity appeared.

projects.title: Heart Disease Prediction
projects.short_description: Classifies patients as at-risk of heart disease from clinical measurements.
projects.problem_statement: Early risk flagging from routine checkup data.
projects.dataset_description: UCI Heart Disease dataset (age, cholesterol, blood pressure, etc.).
projects.business_use_case: Supports doctors in prioritizing follow-up tests for high-risk patients.
projects.workflow_steps: EDA on risk factors -> Handle missing/categorical data -> Train Logistic Regression -> Tune decision threshold -> Evaluate with recall focus -> Explain coefficients
projects.results: Recall = 0.90 on at-risk class, ROC-AUC = 0.91.
projects.key_learning: Chose recall over accuracy since a missed at-risk patient is the costly error.

projects.title: Loan Approval Prediction
projects.short_description: Predicts whether a loan application should be approved.
projects.problem_statement: Speed up loan triage while keeping decisions explainable.
projects.dataset_description: Bank loan applicant dataset with income, credit history, and loan amount.
projects.business_use_case: Gives underwriters an explainable first-pass decision, not a black box.
projects.workflow_steps: Clean applicant data -> Encode categoricals -> Train Decision Tree -> Prune with max_depth/min_samples -> Visualize tree -> Evaluate
projects.results: F1 = 0.84; tree depth limited to 5 for explainability.
projects.key_learning: A shallower, explainable tree was preferred by the (simulated) business stakeholder over a marginally more accurate deep tree.

projects.title: Customer Churn Prediction
projects.short_description: Predicts which telecom customers are likely to cancel their subscription.
projects.problem_statement: Retention teams have limited outreach capacity and need a ranked risk list.
projects.dataset_description: IBM Telco Customer Churn dataset (~7,000 customers).
projects.business_use_case: Lets retention teams target the highest-risk customers first.
projects.workflow_steps: EDA on churn drivers -> Encode & scale -> Baseline Logistic Regression -> Random Forest -> Hyperparameter tuning -> Feature importance -> Deploy as API
projects.results: Recall on churners improved from 55% (baseline) to 81% (tuned RF).
projects.key_learning: Contract type was the single strongest churn driver — a real lever for the business, not just a modeling detail.

projects.title: Customer Segmentation
projects.short_description: Groups customers into behavioral segments from purchase data.
projects.problem_statement: Enables targeted marketing instead of one-size-fits-all campaigns.
projects.dataset_description: E-commerce transaction dataset (RFM: recency, frequency, monetary).
projects.business_use_case: Marketing can tailor offers per segment (e.g., high-value vs. at-risk customers).
projects.workflow_steps: Compute RFM features -> Scale -> Elbow method + silhouette score to pick k -> Fit K-Means -> Profile each cluster -> Visualize with PCA
projects.results: Silhouette score 0.61 with k=4; identified a clear 'high-value loyal' segment worth 40% of revenue.
projects.key_learning: Unsupervised results are only useful once translated into a business narrative per cluster.

projects.title: Credit Card Fraud Detection
projects.short_description: Flags fraudulent transactions in a highly imbalanced dataset.
projects.problem_statement: Every missed fraud is stolen money; every false alarm blocks a real customer.
projects.dataset_description: Kaggle/ULB Credit Card Fraud dataset, 284,807 transactions, 0.17% fraud.
projects.business_use_case: Deployed as a real-time scoring API mimicking a payments pipeline.
projects.workflow_steps: EDA on class imbalance -> Class weighting vs. SMOTE comparison -> Train XGBoost -> Tune threshold on cost matrix -> Deploy via FastAPI + Docker
projects.results: PR-AUC = 0.86; threshold tuned to the business cost tradeoff, not the default 0.5.
projects.key_learning: At extreme imbalance, ROC-AUC is misleadingly high — PR-AUC is the honest scoreboard.

projects.title: Email Spam Classification
projects.short_description: Classifies emails as spam or legitimate.
projects.problem_statement: Reduce inbox clutter and phishing exposure.
projects.dataset_description: SMS Spam Collection / Enron spam dataset.
projects.business_use_case: Filters spam automatically before it reaches an inbox.
projects.workflow_steps: Text cleaning -> TF-IDF vectorization -> Train linear SVM -> Tune C and kernel -> Evaluate
projects.results: F1 = 0.97 on the spam class with a linear kernel.
projects.key_learning: A simple linear SVM on TF-IDF outperformed a more complex RBF kernel here — proved with a direct comparison, not assumed.

projects.title: Movie Recommendation (Similarity Search)
projects.short_description: Recommends similar movies based on user rating patterns.
projects.problem_statement: Improves content discovery and watch-time on a streaming-style platform.
projects.dataset_description: MovieLens 100k dataset.
projects.business_use_case: Drives engagement through 'more like this' recommendations.
projects.workflow_steps: Build user-item matrix -> Normalize ratings -> Fit KNN with cosine similarity -> Generate top-N recommendations -> Evaluate offline
projects.results: Precision@10 = 0.78 on held-out ratings.
projects.key_learning: Item-based KNN was more stable than user-based KNN as the user base grew (fewer cold-start issues).

projects.title: Spam/Disease Diagnosis with Naive Bayes
projects.short_description: Fast baseline text classifier for short medical symptom descriptions.
projects.problem_statement: Quick triage of patient-reported symptoms into likely categories.
projects.dataset_description: Symptom-to-diagnosis text dataset.
projects.business_use_case: Gives a fast, cheap baseline before investing in a heavier deep learning model.
projects.workflow_steps: Clean text -> Bag-of-words -> Train Multinomial Naive Bayes -> Compare against TF-IDF+SVM baseline -> Evaluate
projects.results: F1 = 0.79, trained in under a second — used as the fast baseline for comparison.
projects.key_learning: Naive Bayes is a criminally underrated first baseline: near-zero training cost buys you a real comparison anchor.

projects.title: Dimensionality Reduction for Gene Expression Data
projects.short_description: Reduces thousands of gene-expression features to a handful of components for visualization and downstream modeling.
projects.problem_statement: High-dimensional biological data is unusable directly for visualization or many classifiers.
projects.dataset_description: Public gene expression cancer-subtype dataset.
projects.business_use_case: Lets researchers visualize sample clusters and feed a lower-dimensional input into a classifier.
projects.workflow_steps: Standardize features -> Fit PCA -> Choose components via explained variance -> Visualize in 2D/3D -> Feed into downstream classifier
projects.results: First 10 components explained 92% of variance; downstream classifier accuracy held within 2% of the full-feature model.
projects.key_learning: PCA traded a small accuracy cost for a 50x reduction in feature count and much faster training.

projects.title: Handwritten Digit Recognition
projects.short_description: Classifies handwritten digits (0-9) from pixel data.
projects.problem_statement: The canonical entry point into neural networks, done rigorously with a full comparison.
projects.dataset_description: MNIST (70,000 handwritten digit images).
projects.business_use_case: Illustrates core deep learning workflow before scaling to more complex vision tasks.
projects.workflow_steps: Normalize pixel data -> Build a 2-hidden-layer ANN -> Train with Adam -> Compare against a Logistic Regression baseline -> Evaluate
projects.results: Test accuracy 98.1%, +6 points over the logistic regression baseline.
projects.key_learning: Even a shallow ANN massively outperforms linear models the moment inputs are pixels, not tabular features.

projects.title: Image Classification
projects.short_description: Classifies images into categories using convolutional layers.
projects.problem_statement: Automates visual categorization tasks (e.g., product images, defect vs. no-defect).
projects.dataset_description: CIFAR-10 (60,000 32x32 color images, 10 classes).
projects.business_use_case: Shows the jump from ANN to CNN and quantifies exactly what convolution buys you.
projects.workflow_steps: Data augmentation -> Build CNN (conv/pool/dropout blocks) -> Train -> Compare vs. plain ANN baseline -> Evaluate -> Grad-CAM for interpretability
projects.results: Test accuracy 84% vs. 52% for a plain ANN on the same data.
projects.key_learning: Convolution's parameter sharing is what makes vision tasks tractable — proven here with a direct ANN-vs-CNN comparison.

projects.title: Sentiment Analysis on Reviews
projects.short_description: Classifies product review sentiment using a sequence model.
projects.problem_statement: Monitor brand sentiment at scale instead of manually reading reviews.
projects.dataset_description: IMDB 50k movie reviews.
projects.business_use_case: Automates sentiment monitoring for support/marketing teams.
projects.workflow_steps: Tokenize + pad sequences -> Embedding layer -> Simple RNN baseline -> Compare against LSTM -> Evaluate
projects.results: F1 = 0.85 — used as the baseline the LSTM/GRU project below improves on.
projects.key_learning: Plain RNNs struggle with long reviews due to vanishing gradients — motivates the LSTM version directly.

projects.title: Sentiment Analysis with LSTM/GRU
projects.short_description: Improves review sentiment classification using gated recurrent architectures.
projects.problem_statement: Same business goal as the RNN project — quantifies the improvement gated units provide.
projects.dataset_description: IMDB 50k movie reviews (same dataset as the RNN baseline for a fair comparison).
projects.business_use_case: More reliable sentiment signal for longer-form reviews.
projects.workflow_steps: Reuse RNN preprocessing pipeline -> Swap in LSTM and GRU layers -> Tune sequence length and dropout -> Compare all three architectures side by side
projects.results: LSTM F1 = 0.90 vs. plain RNN's 0.85 on identical data/splits.
projects.key_learning: Gated units earn their complexity specifically on longer sequences — the gap over plain RNN grows with review length.

projects.title: Text Classification with Transformers
projects.short_description: Fine-tunes a pretrained transformer for multi-class text classification.
projects.problem_statement: State-of-the-art accuracy for a text categorization business need (e.g., support ticket topics).
projects.dataset_description: AG News / custom labeled ticket text.
projects.business_use_case: Highest-accuracy option when latency budget allows a transformer in production.
projects.workflow_steps: Tokenize with a pretrained tokenizer -> Fine-tune DistilBERT -> Compare against the LSTM/GRU project -> Evaluate -> Export for serving
projects.results: F1 = 0.94, a 4-point gain over LSTM/GRU at roughly 3x the inference latency.
projects.key_learning: Transformers win on accuracy but the latency cost is real — worth stating the tradeoff instead of defaulting to 'BERT is always better.'

projects.title: Anomaly Detection with Autoencoders
projects.short_description: Detects unusual patterns (e.g., manufacturing defects, unusual transactions) via reconstruction error.
projects.problem_statement: Labeled anomaly data is scarce — autoencoders learn 'normal' and flag what doesn't fit.
projects.dataset_description: Sensor/transaction data with rare, unlabeled anomalies.
projects.business_use_case: Flags anomalies without needing large labeled anomaly datasets.
projects.workflow_steps: Train autoencoder only on normal data -> Compute reconstruction error on holdout -> Set threshold from error distribution -> Evaluate against known anomalies
projects.evaluation_metrics: Precision/Recall on flagged anomalies, Reconstruction Error
projects.results: Recall = 0.88 on injected anomalies at a threshold tuned for <5% false-positive rate.
projects.key_learning: Unsupervised anomaly detection needs a business-defined threshold, not a fixed default — the FP/FN tradeoff has to be a stakeholder decision.

projects.title: Medical Image Classification via Transfer Learning
projects.short_description: Classifies chest X-rays as pneumonia vs. normal using a pretrained backbone.
projects.problem_statement: Assistive triage tool to help radiology prioritize likely-positive scans (not a diagnostic replacement).
projects.dataset_description: Chest X-Ray Images (Pneumonia) dataset, ~5,800 images.
projects.business_use_case: Speeds up radiology triage in a resource-constrained setting.
projects.workflow_steps: Train a CNN from scratch as baseline -> Fine-tune ResNet50/EfficientNet -> Data augmentation -> Threshold tuned for recall -> Add Grad-CAM for interpretability
projects.results: Recall improved from 84% (from-scratch CNN) to 95% (transfer learning).
projects.key_learning: Transfer learning's real advantage shows up precisely when labeled data is limited, as in most medical imaging problems.

projects.title: Personal AI Assistant
projects.short_description: A conversational agent that manages reminders, answers questions, and drafts messages.
projects.problem_statement: Reduce time spent on routine personal admin tasks.
projects.dataset_description: N/A — built on live conversation + tool APIs (calendar, notes).
projects.business_use_case: Demonstrates tool-calling and multi-turn memory in an LLM agent.
projects.workflow_steps: Define tool schema (calendar, notes, search) -> System prompt + persona design -> LLM function-calling loop -> Add short-term memory -> Test multi-turn tasks
projects.results: 18/20 realistic multi-step tasks completed correctly in manual testing.
projects.key_learning: Most failures were tool-argument formatting, not reasoning — solved with stricter schema validation before the call.

projects.title: AI Research Assistant
projects.short_description: Given a topic, searches, reads, and synthesizes multiple sources into a structured summary.
projects.problem_statement: Cuts hours of manual literature/web research down to minutes.
projects.dataset_description: Web search results + scraped articles per query.
projects.business_use_case: Analysts get a sourced first-draft summary instead of starting from a blank page.
projects.workflow_steps: Query decomposition -> Web search tool calls -> Scrape + chunk content -> Summarize per source -> Synthesize final report with citations
projects.tools_used: Python, LangChain, vector store, search API
projects.results: 92% of citations verified as correctly attributed to source content.
projects.key_learning: Forcing the agent to cite verbatim snippets (not paraphrase) before synthesizing cut hallucinated claims sharply.

projects.title: Resume Screening AI Agent
projects.short_description: Screens resumes against a job description and ranks candidates with reasoning.
projects.problem_statement: Speeds up first-pass recruiting triage for high-volume roles.
projects.dataset_description: Sample resumes + job description dataset (synthetic/anonymized).
projects.business_use_case: Recruiters get a ranked shortlist with an explanation, not just a score.
projects.workflow_steps: Parse resume text -> Extract structured fields (skills, experience) -> Embed JD + resume -> LLM scoring with rationale -> Rank candidates
projects.results: 81% top-5 overlap with a human recruiter's shortlist on a held-out test set.
projects.key_learning: The rationale text mattered more to end users than the score itself — recruiters wanted to see *why*, not just a number.

projects.title: Document Q&A Agent (RAG)
projects.short_description: Answers questions grounded in a set of uploaded documents.
projects.problem_statement: Lets non-technical users query internal documents (policies, manuals) in plain English.
projects.dataset_description: Company policy PDFs / manuals (sample set).
projects.business_use_case: Reduces time employees spend searching internal documentation.
projects.workflow_steps: Chunk documents -> Embed + store in vector DB -> Retrieve top-k chunks per query -> LLM answers with citations -> Guard against hallucination with 'answer only from context' prompting
projects.results: 89% of answers verified correct and grounded in the cited chunk.
projects.key_learning: Chunk size/overlap tuning mattered more to answer quality than the choice of LLM.

projects.title: RAG-based Agent over a Codebase
projects.short_description: Answers questions about a codebase by retrieving relevant files/functions before answering.
projects.problem_statement: Helps new developers or recruiters understand a codebase without reading it all.
projects.dataset_description: This portfolio's own backend/frontend code as the retrieval corpus.
projects.business_use_case: Demonstrates RAG applied to code, not just prose documents.
projects.workflow_steps: Chunk code by function/file -> Embed with a code-aware embedding model -> Retrieve relevant chunks -> LLM answers with file/line references
projects.results: Correctly located the relevant file/function in 9/10 test questions about this repo.
projects.key_learning: Chunking by function boundary (not fixed character length) noticeably improved retrieval precision for code.

projects.title: Data Analysis Agent
projects.short_description: Takes a natural-language question about a dataset and returns an answer plus a chart.
projects.problem_statement: Lets non-technical stakeholders query data without writing SQL/pandas themselves.
projects.dataset_description: Sample sales/business CSV dataset.
projects.business_use_case: Cuts the ad-hoc analytics request queue for a data team.
projects.workflow_steps: Parse question -> Generate pandas/SQL code via LLM -> Execute safely in a sandboxed runner -> Auto-generate a chart -> Return answer + chart
projects.results: 85% of natural-language queries produced a correct answer and chart on the first try.
projects.key_learning: Sandboxing generated code (no arbitrary exec) was the non-negotiable engineering piece, not an afterthought.

projects.title: Customer Support Agent
projects.short_description: Answers common customer questions and escalates complex ones to a human.
projects.problem_statement: Reduces first-response time and human workload on repetitive tickets.
projects.dataset_description: Sample support ticket + FAQ dataset.
projects.business_use_case: Handles the long tail of repetitive tickets so agents focus on complex cases.
projects.workflow_steps: Intent classification -> RAG over FAQ/knowledge base -> Confidence-based escalation to human -> Log all conversations for review
projects.results: 62% of tickets fully resolved by the agent without human escalation.
projects.key_learning: A conservative escalation threshold (when in doubt, hand off to a human) built more user trust than maximizing deflection rate.

projects.title: Multi-Agent Research & Report Workflow
projects.short_description: Coordinates multiple specialized agents (researcher, writer, reviewer) to produce a final report.
projects.problem_statement: Complex tasks benefit from role specialization the way a human team would divide work.
projects.dataset_description: N/A — orchestration project over LLM agents and tool calls.
projects.business_use_case: Shows agent orchestration beyond a single-agent chatbot.
projects.workflow_steps: Define agent roles and handoff protocol -> Researcher agent gathers info -> Writer agent drafts -> Reviewer agent critiques and requests revisions -> Final output
projects.results: Reviewer-agent loop caught factual/formatting issues in 70% of first drafts before human review.
projects.key_learning: A dedicated reviewer agent with a narrow checklist caught more issues than asking one agent to 'double check itself.'

projects.title: Automated Power BI Report Refresh Notification
projects.short_description: Automatically checks Power BI dataset refresh status and notifies stakeholders on failure.
projects.problem_statement: Manual process: someone had to open Power BI daily to check if the refresh succeeded.
projects.dataset_description: Manually checked refresh status daily, delayed failure discovery by up to 24 hours.
projects.business_use_case: Python script polls Power BI REST API for refresh status and posts to Slack/Teams/email on failure.
projects.workflow_steps: Call Power BI REST API -> Parse refresh status -> On failure, send alert (email/Slack) -> Schedule via cron/Task Scheduler -> Log history
projects.tools_used: Python, Power BI REST API, Slack/Teams webhook
projects.results: Failure detection time dropped from ~24 hours to under 15 minutes.
projects.key_learning: Reliable alerting infrastructure mattered more than the automation script itself — silent failures are worse than the manual process.

projects.title: Excel Data Cleaning Automation
projects.short_description: Automates cleaning of messy, recurring Excel exports (formatting, duplicates, missing values).
projects.problem_statement: Analysts spent hours each week manually cleaning the same recurring report format.
projects.dataset_description: Manual cleanup in Excel, ~3 hours/week, error-prone and inconsistent between analysts.
projects.business_use_case: Script standardizes column names, removes duplicates, fixes types, and outputs a clean file automatically.
projects.workflow_steps: Define cleaning rules from historical manual edits -> Build a reusable Python cleaning pipeline (openpyxl/pandas) -> Validate against manual output -> Schedule to run on file arrival
projects.results: Cut weekly cleaning time from ~3 hours to under 5 minutes; eliminated inconsistent manual edits.
projects.key_learning: Codifying the analyst's implicit cleaning rules was the real work — the script itself was straightforward once rules were explicit.

projects.title: Email Classification Automation
projects.short_description: Automatically classifies and routes incoming emails to the right team/folder.
projects.problem_statement: Support inbox required manual triage before routing to the right team.
projects.dataset_description: Manually read and forwarded each email, ~2 minutes per email.
projects.business_use_case: NLP classifier reads incoming email subject/body and auto-routes/labels it.
projects.workflow_steps: Collect labeled historical emails -> Train text classifier (TF-IDF + Logistic Regression) -> Integrate with email API (IMAP/Gmail API) -> Auto-label/route on arrival
projects.results: Routing accuracy 91%; manual triage time reduced by ~80%.
projects.key_learning: A simple classical ML model was sufficient here — the automation/integration plumbing was the harder half of the project, not the model.

projects.title: Invoice Data Extraction Automation
projects.short_description: Extracts structured fields (vendor, amount, date) from PDF/scanned invoices automatically.
projects.problem_statement: Finance team manually retyped invoice data into the accounting system.
projects.dataset_description: Manual data entry from PDF invoices, ~5 minutes per invoice, occasional typos.
projects.business_use_case: OCR + information extraction pipeline reads invoices and populates structured records automatically.
projects.workflow_steps: OCR text extraction (Tesseract) -> Layout-aware field extraction -> Validate against known formats -> Push structured data to accounting system API
projects.results: Field extraction accuracy 94%; processing time per invoice cut from 5 minutes to ~10 seconds.
projects.key_learning: Layout-aware extraction (not just raw OCR text) was necessary once invoice formats varied across vendors.

projects.title: Web Scraping for Market Data
projects.short_description: Scrapes competitor pricing/market data on a schedule and compiles it into a report.
projects.problem_statement: Analysts manually checked competitor websites for pricing changes.
projects.dataset_description: Manual weekly price checks across multiple competitor sites.
projects.business_use_case: Scheduled scraper collects pricing data and flags changes automatically.
projects.workflow_steps: Build site-specific scrapers (requests/BeautifulSoup, Selenium for JS-heavy pages) -> Store data with timestamps -> Diff against previous run -> Alert on significant changes -> Schedule via cron
projects.results: Weekly manual task automated to a nightly run; price-change detection latency cut from days to hours.
projects.key_learning: Handling site structure changes gracefully (retries + alerting on scrape failure) mattered as much as the scraping logic itself.

projects.title: Data Pipeline Automation for Daily Reporting
projects.short_description: Automates the daily ETL process feeding a business reporting dashboard.
projects.problem_statement: Someone manually ran extraction/cleaning scripts each morning before reports were usable.
projects.dataset_description: Manual daily ETL run, prone to being skipped or run late.
projects.business_use_case: Orchestrated pipeline extracts, transforms, and loads data on a fixed schedule with failure alerts.
projects.workflow_steps: Define ETL steps -> Orchestrate with Airflow/cron -> Add data quality checks -> Alert on failure -> Log run history
projects.results: On-time report delivery improved from ~70% to 99% of days.
projects.key_learning: Automated data-quality checks caught two real upstream data issues before they reached the dashboard.

projects.title: Workflow Automation for Approvals
projects.short_description: Automates a multi-step approval workflow (request -> review -> approval -> notification).
projects.problem_statement: Manual email-chain approvals were slow and easy to lose track of.
projects.dataset_description: Manual email-based approval chain, average 3-day turnaround.
projects.business_use_case: Automated workflow tracks state, routes to the right approver, and notifies all parties on status change.
projects.workflow_steps: Model workflow states -> Build routing logic -> Integrate notifications (email/Slack) -> Add audit log -> Deploy as a lightweight internal tool
projects.results: Average approval turnaround cut from 3 days to under 6 hours.
projects.key_learning: A visible audit trail was the feature stakeholders valued most — more than the speed improvement itself.

projects.title: HR Recruitment Screening Automation
projects.short_description: Automates initial resume screening and interview scheduling for high-volume roles.
projects.problem_statement: Recruiters manually screened hundreds of resumes and coordinated scheduling by email.
projects.dataset_description: Manual resume review + back-and-forth scheduling emails, days of recruiter time per role.
projects.business_use_case: Automated pipeline pre-screens resumes and auto-schedules interviews for qualified candidates.
projects.workflow_steps: Parse resumes -> Rule/ML-based pre-screening -> Auto-send scheduling link to qualified candidates -> Sync to calendar -> Notify recruiter of shortlist
projects.results: Recruiter screening time reduced ~65%; average time-to-schedule cut from 4 days to same-day.
projects.key_learning: Automating scheduling (not just screening) is where most of the actual time savings came from.

projects.title: Customer Support Ticket Automation
projects.short_description: Automates ticket tagging, prioritization, and routing for a support inbox.
projects.problem_statement: Support ops manually tagged and prioritized every incoming ticket before routing.
projects.dataset_description: Manual triage of all incoming tickets, inconsistent prioritization between shifts.
projects.business_use_case: Automation tags, scores urgency, and routes each ticket the moment it arrives.
projects.workflow_steps: Classify ticket category -> Score urgency from keywords/SLA rules -> Auto-route to the right queue -> Notify on high-urgency tickets -> Log for reporting
projects.results: Manual triage time eliminated; urgent-ticket response time improved by 45%.
projects.key_learning: This project became the seed for the full 'Support Ticket Auto-Router' capstone in the Automation + AI Agent overlap.
